chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 115 – The Nazool (Arrival) of Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup>, may the Salawat of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> be upon them<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن موقوف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (354/ 5)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن موقوف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (354/ 5)
text: خَرَجَ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) فِي بَعْضِ عُمَرِهِ وَ مَعَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ وُلْدِ الزُّبَيْرِ كَانَ يَقُولُ بِإِمَامَتِهِ فَنَزَلُوا فِي مَنْهَلٍ مِنْ تِلْكَ الْمَنَاهِلِ تَحْتَ نَخْلٍ يَابِسٍ قَدْ يَبِسَ مِنَ الْعَطَشِ

فَفُرِشَ لِلْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) تَحْتَ نَخْلَةٍ وَ فُرِشَ لِلزُّبَيْرِيِّ بِحِذَاهُ تَحْتَ نَخْلَةٍ أُخْرَى قَالَ فَقَالَ الزُّبَيْرِيُّ وَ رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ لَوْ كَانَ فِي هَذَا النَّخْلِ رُطَبٌ لَأَكَلْنَا مِنْهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ الْحَسَنُ وَ إِنَّكَ لَتَشْتَهِي الرُّطَبَ فَقَالَ الزُّبَيْرِيُّ نَعَمْ

قَالَ فَرَفَعَ يَدَهُ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ فَدَعَا بِكَلَامٍ لَمْ أَفْهَمْهُ فَاخْضَرَّتِ النَّخْلَةُ ثُمَّ صَارَتْ إِلَى حَالِهَا فَأَوْرَقَتْ وَ حَمَلَتْ رُطَباً فَقَالَ الْجَمَّالُ الَّذِي اكْتَرَوْا مِنْهُ سِحْرٌ وَ اللَّهِ قَالَ فَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ ( عليه السلام ) وَيْلَكَ لَيْسَ بِسِحْرٍ وَ لَكِنْ دَعْوَةُ ابْنِ نَبِيٍّ مُسْتَجَابَةٌ قَالَ فَصَعِدُوا إِلَى النَّخْلَةِ فَصَرَمُوا مَا كَانَ فِيهِ فَكَفَاهُمْ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya and Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from al Qasim Al Nahdy, from Ismail Bin Mihran, from Al Kunasy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: Once ‘Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> went outside (the town) with him<sup>asws</sup> was a man from the sons of Al-Zubeyr who was saying (believing) in his<sup>asws</sup> Imamate. So they encamped in an oasis from those oasis beneath a dry palm tree which had dried from the drought.

So it was furnished (carpeted) for Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> beneath the palm tree, and its was furnished for Al-Zubeyri by the side beneath another palm tree. So Al-Zubeyri said, and raised his head, ‘If only there were dates in this palm tree, we could have eaten from it’. So Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> said to him: ‘And you are desiring the dates?’ So Al-Zubeyri said, ‘Yes’.

He (Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said): ‘So he<sup>asws</sup> raised his<sup>asws</sup> hands towards the sky and supplicated with a speech which he did not understand, and the palm tree turned green. Then it came to be to its state whereby it sprouted leaves and bore fruit. So the cameleer whom they had hired camels from, said, ‘Sorcery, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>!’ So Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Woe be unto you! It isn’t sorcery. But, a supplication of the son<sup>asws</sup> of a Prophet<sup>azwj</sup> that is Answered’. So, they climbed up the palm tree and they picked whatever was in it, and it sufficed them’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people has narrated has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Ni‘man from Sayf ibn ‘Amira from abu Bakr al-Hadrami has said that Jumhu‘ada daughter of ’Ash‘th ibn Qays al-Kidi poisoned al-Hassan ibn Ali (a.s.) and a female servant of the Imam (a.s.). The female servant, however, vomited the poison but in the case of al-Hassan (a.s.) the poison remained his digestive system and caused swelling that killed him.”
